name: Artie Real-Time CDC API
what_it_does: Real-time CDC (change data capture) platform with REST API — enabling data teams to stream database changes from PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MongoDB into data warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, Databricks) with sub-minute latency, using log-based CDC to avoid database load, with automatic schema evolution and no-code connector configuration.
use_cases: Streaming database changes via Artie CDC for real-time data warehouse sync from PostgreSQL
Replicating MySQL binlog via Artie for sub-minute latency analytics warehouse population
Handling schema evolution via Artie for automatic DDL change propagation to destinations
Monitoring CDC pipelines via Artie API for replication lag and health observability
Configuring table replication via Artie API for selective column and table sync management
not_for: Batch/scheduled ELT (use Fivetran or Airbyte for non-real-time requirements)
Teams needing open-source self-hosted CDC (Artie is SaaS-only)
best_when: A data team needs real-time, sub-minute CDC replication from operational databases (PostgreSQL/MySQL) to a data warehouse without managing Debezium or Kafka infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need batch/scheduled ELT, have sources beyond OLTP databases, or require open-source self-hosted CDC pipelines.

auth.notes: API key via Authorization: Bearer header. API key from Artie dashboard settings. REST API at api.artie.com/v1/. Deployment ID required for deployment-level operations. Source and destination credentials stored securely in Artie. SaaS-only: no self-hosted option. CDC uses database replication slots (PostgreSQL) or binlog (MySQL) — requires DB-level configuration.

pricing.paid_tiers: Usage-based pricing per GB replicated; Enterprise custom pricing
pricing.notes: US company (San Francisco). Founded 2022 by Tony Vu and Jacqueline Cheong. Y Combinator W22. $22M+ funding. Real-time CDC focus: sub-minute latency from source to destination. Log-based CDC: reads WAL (PostgreSQL) or binlog (MySQL) — minimal database load vs. polling. Sources: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB. Destinations: Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, Databricks. Schema evolution: automatic column addition/type change handling. No Kafka required: Artie manages the streaming infrastructure. Merge strategy: UPSERT with soft deletes or hard deletes configurable.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Bearer token. Deployment ID required. Log-based CDC (WAL/binlog). SaaS-only. US company. SOC2. Sub-minute latency. PostgreSQL/MySQL/MongoDB sources. Snowflake/BigQuery/Redshift/Databricks destinations.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: CDC is inherently idempotent: log-based replication with sequence numbers. API reads idempotent. Deployment configuration: idempotent by deployment ID. Table sync: check current state before modifying.
known_agent_gotchas: Database prerequisites: PostgreSQL needs wal_level=logical and replication slot created; MySQL needs binlog_format=ROW
Deployment ID: required for all deployment-level API operations — get from Artie dashboard
Source credentials: Artie needs a replication user with REPLICATION privilege (PostgreSQL) or REPLICATION SLAVE (MySQL)
Schema evolution: Artie handles most DDL automatically but some destructive operations (DROP COLUMN) need configuration
SaaS-only: no self-hosted option — data flows through Artie's infrastructure between source and destination
Latency vs. batch: Artie is real-time CDC — not for teams that only need daily/hourly batch syncs

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Bearer token API key. SOC2 certified. Source DB credentials stored in Artie — significant trust surface (replication user has broad read access). Log-based CDC: reads WAL/binlog not live queries — reduces attack surface vs. polling. US data residency. Replication user: use least-privilege replication-only account. Network: consider VPC peering or IP allowlist for database access from Artie.
